w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: The project lives at D:\Projects\Personal_Projects\Websites\fitosys. Every file and folder below is relative to this root.
app/ # Next.js App Router — all pages and API routes
lib/ # Business logic, API clients, utilities
types/ # TypeScript interfaces and type definitions
styles/ # Global CSS (minimal — Tailwind handles most)
# Template for env vars — commit this
2. app/ Directory — Pages and API Routes
All routes are collocated under app/. Pages use
(auth)/ # Route group — auth pages (no dashboard layout)
# Home: stat cards, attention list, renewals
3. lib/ Directory — Business Logic and API Clients
lib/ contains all non-UI logic. Nothing in lib/ should import from app/ or components/. Every external service has its own subfolder.
# Raw Meta Graph API call — never called directly by
# Named builder functions — one per template
# Gemini API client — model config, retry logic
# Scores client churn risk from check-in data
# One-line coaching priority for the week
# Throws if no session — use in API routes
# Date helpers: add weeks, format IST, get week number
All components are in TypeScript. No default exports except for
files. Every component has a single responsibility.
# Reusable stat card: label + value + optional trend
# Clients needing attention — name, reason, actions
# Upcoming renewals — client, program, days remaining
# Last week AI summary preview card
# Program display: name, price, duration, toggle
# Full AI summary display with sections
# Radio group: coach's active programs + prices
# Loads checkout.js, opens modal, handles success/fail
# Consistent page title + subtitle + optional action
All DB table interfaces live in types/
. All API request/response shapes live in types/
# Request/response shapes for all API routes
: string | null // nullable until webhook fires
# Dev seed data (2 coaches, 5 clients each)
8. Module Boundaries — What Can Import What
This is the most important section for keeping the codebase clean as it grows. Violating these boundaries creates circular dependencies and untestable code.
lib/* only. Never from components/. Never from other
components/*. hooks/*. types/*. Never from lib/ directly — use API routes.
components/intake/*. components/shared/*. types/*. Never from lib/ directly.
/*. hooks/*. types/*. Never from lib/*. Never from app/.
types/*. Nothing else from the project.
Nothing — types are leaf nodes. No circular imports.
Golden rule: data flows upward. lib/ has no knowledge of app/ or components/.
If an API route needs to call another API route's logic, extract that logic into lib/ and call it from both.
MVP does not require full test coverage. These are the minimum tests required before each deployment.
Zero errors required before every commit. Non-negotiable.
webhook: valid signature accepted, invalid rejected with 400.
Coach A cannot query Coach B's clients,
Confirm WhatsApp message arrives on registered test number.
Full flow: intake form → order creation → test payment → webhook → client in DB → WhatsApp received.
